questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 2 inches and a height of 6 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = π(2)²(6) = π(4)(6) = 24π in³.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 3 meters. What is its volume in terms of π?
A cone has a radius of 4 cm and a height of 9 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
A rectangular prism has a volume of 120 cubic units. If its length is 10 units and width is 3 units, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height, so 120 = 10 × 3 × h; therefore h = 120 ÷ 30 = 4 units.

questions.question: A cylinder has a height of 5 inches and a volume of 45π cubic inches. What is its radius?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h, so 45π = πr²(5); divide by 5π to get r² = 9, thus r = 3 inches.
